item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F-dlJekbXYo
I loved this scip. Great amount of body horror and an GOI from Russia. +1
E: Also, thanks for referencing one of my favorite bands, Circus Contraption, in the title. You are awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for harlan ellison

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Victims' legs are heavily scarred upon removal, but the actual mechanical process is a ~~mystery~~!

I wanted a name that sounded fairly archaic, to hint at the age of the artifact and the article itself. But if you or anyone else wants to suggest a different name that's suitably old-sounding while still being recognizably Russian, I'd be happy to use it in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was not familiar with Tom Waits' work when I started writing Herman Fuller articles, but after seeing him mentioned in article comments a few times, I decided to look him up. I'm a huge fan now. Thanks for that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanted the schematics to be written in Russian, but since I'm not familiar with the language at all, I just grabbed some random text off an RU article I had open in another tab. I purposefully reduced the image to a smaller size so readers familiar with the language wouldn't be able to instantly recognize that the text was used completely out of context.

The article I borrowed from was 1079-RU, if I remember correctly, though it may have been 1016-RU. It was definitely a Meat Circus article, I remember that much.

I salute user Slithy's superb detective work on the matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cameras are crushed by the extension and activation of the interior mechanisms.

So does it make a puree out of the lower part of the body or mangle it temporarily?

It properly translates to Lelyud, but could be transliterated as Leljud.

Well, I am a Russian citizen and even I couldn't recognize it as written. It's translated as yu as per foreign passport rules. ju is outdated.

it can be used however instead of yu after yer to indicate that for example the word дизъюнкция is read [d'izju…] and not [d'iz'u]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) Cameras are crushed by the extension and activation of the interior mechanisms.

2) How does it turn people into flippin' meatworms? It's an anomalous nightmare machine It alters the victim's anatomy in such a way that the subject's breath is drawn from a vent at the rear of the machine.

3) Лелюд is a Russian name derived from "Lenin loves children". It properly translates to Lelyud, but could be transliterated as Leljud. Before joining Herman Fuller's crew, Mr. Kozlovsky contributed to the Meat Circus as a hobby when he wasn't busy constructing machines for the USSR. His English wasn't the best when he first came to the states, hence the slight imprecision of the romanization of his name. He never bothered to change it since nobody at the circus called him by his proper name anyway. Someone called him Charley at one point and the name stuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
three things

why won't we stick a camera into the enclosed portion of the device to see what happens to the lower part of the person?

how does this thing breathe? does it have a hole instead of a mouth?

and more importantly Leljud is not a real name

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh good Lord this is fucking horrific.

So much squick, so much horror, so much funny.

You already had me at 'meat circus', whoever coined that. One of those brilliant phrases that takes innocuous (well, reasonably. I'm vegetarian and dislike circuses intensely!) words and makes them so awful just by their juxtaposition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did I miss this? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's at the very end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the Circus of the Disquieting. It's a Tom Waits album given life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "I have no mouth and ice must cream" was funny and the exploding confetti guy was just…well, that was pretty unique. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What point of game is the Meat Circus in? I'm a decent way through the game but I'm not sure if I've gotten there yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the…

Okay, I don't like this but it's so frickin wierd and well written i can't help but upvote.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there a bigger version of the first picture? The only small print I can decipher is "center … must be placed …" and "Cell cleanup should". The guy who came up with the Meat Circus idea is curious too.
UPD: Found the original words. Will probably post 1016-RU here sometime soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit that when I first saw the Meat Circus reference I was worried that this was going to be difficult to understand for those of us who haven't read the Russian wiki, but I feel like the article does a good job of avoiding that. Overall, I feel like I really enjoyed the article, especially the addenda.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost forgot to mention that the title of this skip, Circus Contraption, is a reference to a musical group of the same name. Their music is totally insane and has provided some great inspiration for me while writing about the Circus of the Disquieting. You can listen to one of their albums for free on bandcamp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One complaint: "secrete" and "excrete" are not interchangeable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how 1695-2 subjects are not permanently transformed. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this should have the "ectoentropic" tag, since when the "worm" is extended it gains mass from out of nowhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
PeppersGhost, what the fuck man? What dark places do you visit in order to write this stuff?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this because it's creepy, yet not as horrifying as some other transformation skips, yet completely horrifying in other ways.

I had to stare at this

No Mouth And Ice Must Cream

for quite some time and just contemplate its beauty.

Also I love how the guy killed himself at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was already interested. And then I got to the confetti. I couldn't upvote hard enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I reached the end of the second interview log, I said "What the fuck?" out loud and and was briefly confused by having to scroll up to find the rating module so I could upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They are kept alive by the sound of laughter and the love of children.

and also by a myriad of metal instruments poking them where the sun doesn't shine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor quibble: how do 1695-2 subjects breathe if their nose and mouth are gone? Wouldn't they suffocate?

Otherwise, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah, damn you Circus. Why you so interesting.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instant-vote when I read Herman Fuller. I completely love this!

E: I owe you a soul for this Pepper…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: PeppersGhost … Where do you get your acid, bro?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Squicky effect that makes sense in context, well-executed interviews. Manages to get in lots of WTF while still sticking together. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…I don't even…What the Fuck?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you're sayin'. Edited accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor quibble:

If there are two significant components of this SCP, then they should be categorized as -1 and -2, with "SCP-1695" referring to the entirety of the SCP. It bothers me when XXXX is the machine and XXXX-# are what it produces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first thought was of Psychonauts as well. But I don't think it is enough of an issue to need a change.

For one thing, the game imagery it brought to mind was quite apt. Herman Fuller's Circus is definitely a bad place for bunnies!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man with the changes it is much stronger and a hearty +1 for you my friend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yesyesyesyesyes.
You are officially my favorite author.
And that first interview log… That could have supported the article by itself.
Upvoted with the strength of a thousand suns, plus a black dwarf.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only quibble is that the footnotes explaining what the distal phalanges are seem slightly out of place in a scientific article where, presumably, people are familiar with basic human anatomy. But that's alright because most of our readers have probably long since forgotten that segment of human biology class. I upvoted it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. Impressively gross in concept, and well-described. +1.

However, I must express some slight reservation about the name "Meat Circus" for a GoI, since "Meat Circus" is fairly well known as the name of a level on the videogame "Psychonauts". I know you're not the originator of that name, as you linked back to it on the Russian wiki, but it maybe needs thinking about over there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So Meat Circus, + Meat of Evil.

I can shiggety diggety.

No Mouth And Ice Must Cream

Oh I see what you did there…

shortly afterward Mr. Kozlovsky removed his shoes and socks without the use of his hands (as they were bound behind his back), pinched his tongue between the toes of his left foot, and extracted his tongue from his mouth.

OH HELLO BEST NEW GOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I originally set out to do these Circus skips, I planned it to be a series of 6 SCP articles. However, after I wrote the first three, I decided that I really wanted to have a crossover between the Circus Of The Disquieting and the RU wiki's Meat Circus, thus bumping the overall number of skips in the series to 7. Three more to go!

Special thanks to Voct, Soulless, and anqxyr for their feedback on the drafts!

In 2019, I changed the second image to Homeless by Brandon. The first picture is my own.

Discover more of my weird ramblings in
~PeppersGhost's Gallery of Illusion!~
